content_markdown: Methane and carbon dioxide are both greenhouse gases that are believed to cause global warming. The reason for this is that these gases

diagram_metadata.description: No diagram provided in the image.

options.label: A
options.text: absorb incoming radiation from the Sun.

options.label: B
options.text: transmit the incoming radiation from the Sun and radiation from the Earth.

options.label: C
options.text: reflect incoming radiation from the Sun.

options.label: D
options.text: transmit incoming radiation from the Sun and absorb outgoing radiation from the Earth.

vibe_explanation: Greenhouse gases are characterized by their ability to allow short-wavelength radiation (visible light) from the Sun to pass through the atmosphere (transmission), while absorbing long-wavelength infrared radiation emitted by the Earth's surface. This trapped energy warms the atmosphere.
